figures.caption: TABLE III: Multi-seed statistical significance (5 seeds). Each row compares a method against LDA using a paired t-test. ∆: mean accuracy difference (method − LDA). Bold p-values indicate statistical significance at α = 0.05. We show all six comparison methods; the pattern is consistent across all four backbones (see supplementary for full table).

TABLE II: Classification accuracy (%, mean±std over 5 seeds) and total time (seconds) on Tiny ImageNet across four backbones. Formatting follows Table I. All reduction methods project to d = 199 dimensions.

TABLE I: Classification accuracy (%, mean±std over 5 seeds) and total time (seconds) on CIFAR-100 across four backbones. The best result per backbone is bolded; the second best is underlined. All reduction methods project to d = 99 dimensions (C − 1) except RDA variants which use 99+k. Methods marked with † are single-seed results.
